front_line: Data center cooling systems for the AI buildout boom
thesis: Every new data center becomes recurring revenue for 5-7 years as AI chips get hotter. But consecutive earnings misses of 15-35% while trading at 89x free cash flow suggests they're losing the race against commoditization despite massive demand.
moat: Switching costs only apply to replacements, not new construction
risks: Consecutive quarters of 15-35% earnings misses during supposed demand boom

drafts.x_post: SOLS: AI data centers need cooling, but this company keeps missing earnings by 15-35% while trading at 89x FCF. The physics problem is real, the execution isn't. Watching for Q2 2026 revenue growth below 8% to confirm they can't capitalize fast enough.
video_talking_points: Solstice makes cooling systems for data centers during the biggest AI buildout in history.
Every chip gets hotter as AI advances, creating a physics problem that only gets worse.
Each new contract becomes recurring revenue for 5-7 years as data centers expand.
They've missed earnings by 15-35% two quarters straight with $1.77B debt against only $142M cash flow.
The market opportunity is massive but they're executing poorly at 89x free cash flow.
Watching this one until they prove they can capitalize on the AI boom without breaking their balance sheet.

image.query: Computer or data center COOLING (something related to cooling and AI)
